column2: Local hostname/device that emitted the log; if not present in audit records, populate from collector/agent metadata.
Audit record category (e.g., USER_LOGIN, USER_CMD, SYSCALL); primary cue for aligning equivalent audit events.
Audit event identifier derived from msg=audit(time:serial); groups multi-record bundles into one logical event.
Primary account name referenced by the event (from acct); human-readable identity for attribution alignment.
Original authenticated identity (Audit User ID); stable initiator anchor across sudo/su and privilege transitions.
Active/effective user ID at the time of the event; captures current privilege context (may differ from auid).
Audit session identifier (from ses); groups actions within the same login/session context.
Normalized controlling TTY (e.g., pts/1); distinguishes interactive sessions vs non-interactive/background execution.
Raw terminal/channel field as logged (may overlap with tty); retained to preserve source semantics.
Remote/source IP when present (from addr); aligns ingress origin with auth/dns where applicable.
Remote/source hostname when present (from hostname); distinct from local host that emitted the log.
Process ID that generated the record; supports short-range chaining of related actions.
Parent process ID; enables process-tree provenance (who spawned whom).
Full executable path (from exe); higher fidelity than process_name for identifying the true binary.
Short process name (from comm); stable token for grouping by program family.
Full argv/proctitle (decoded); captures invocation parameters for behavior-equivalence alignment.
Recorded command payload when available (decoded); aligns action intent across audit/auth.
Current working directory; execution context signal (e.g., web directory vs home directory).
Kernel syscall identifier when present; low-level action primitive for precise behavior alignment.
Outcome indicator from audit (e.g., res=success/failure); supports cross-log success/failure alignment.
